2605.00414.title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
2605.00414.paper_summary: Decision trees and diffusion models are ostensibly disparate model classes, one discrete and hierarchical, the other continuous and dynamic. This work unifies the two by establishing a crisp mathematical correspondence between hierarchical decision trees and diffusion processes in appropriate limiting regimes. Our unification reveals a shared optimization principle: \emph{Global Trajectory Score Matching (GTSM)}, for which gradient boosting (in an idealized version) is asymptotically optimal. We underscore the conceptual value of our work through two key practical instantiations: \treeflow, which achieves competitive generation quality on tabular data with higher fidelity and a 2\times computational speedup, and \dsmtree, a novel distillation method that transfers hierarchical decision logic into neural networks, matching teacher performance within 2\% on many benchmarks.
2605.00414.paper_summary_zh: 決策樹和擴散模型表面上是兩種不同的模型類別，一種是離散的和層次化的，另一種是連續的和動態的。這項工作通過在適當的極限範疇中建立層次決策樹和擴散過程之間的明確數學對應來統一這兩者。我們的統一揭示了一個共同的優化原則：\emph{全球軌跡分數匹配 (GTSM)}，對於此原則，梯度提升（在理想化版本中）是漸近最優的。我們通過兩個關鍵的實踐實例強調了我們工作的概念價值：\treeflow，它在表格數據上實現了具有更高保真度的競爭生成質量和2\times的計算加速，以及\dsmtree，一種新穎的蒸餾方法，將層次決策邏輯轉移到神經網絡中，在許多基準上與教師的表現相匹配，誤差在2\%以內。
2605.00414.author: Sai Niranjan Ramachandran et.al.
